WARNING: These test points are for A2338_M2 (820-02773) ONLY. For other boards, use that board's diagnostic files.
title: 20V DCPS Triage — Board Powers But No Display / No Boot
description: Step-by-step diagnostic for A2338_M2 MacBook Pro 13" that draws power but fails to boot or show display. Uses 20V DCPS to bypass PD negotiation. Board has DFR (Touch Bar).
prerequisites: Board has already passed 5V triage (PPBUS_AON, PP3V8_AON, PP5V_S2 all present)

scenarios.range: Board powers but no boot at 20V
scenarios.meaning: Board draws power at 20V but fails to boot to display. PMU may be partially up. Need to trace which power domain has failed.

steps.action: Verify PPBUS_AON at 20V input: Apply 20V via USB-C. Check PPBUS_AON at PBUS caps.
steps.measure: Apply 20V via USB-C. Check PPBUS_AON at PBUS caps.
steps.expected: 12.6V (with battery) or rising voltage toward PPBUS regulation point
steps.if_missing: too_high: Battery disconnected and charger not regulating — check SYS_DETECT; too_low: Charger IC U5200 not switching properly; current_draw_high: Short on PPBUS_AON — diode mode PBUS caps

steps.action: Check all S2 rails: Measure S2-state rails that should be present after PMU startup.
steps.measure: PP1V25_S2 (1.25V) from MPMU BUCK 13 [page 109]; PP1V21_S2 (1.21V) from MPMU BUCK 3 [page 108]; PP1V12_S2M_AMPH (1.12V) from SPMU BUCK 14 [page 109] ... and 3 more
steps.if_missing: Specific PMU buck/LDO has failed. Check that specific output for short.

steps.action: Check S1 retention rails: Measure S1 rails. These should come up before SoC awake state.
steps.measure: PP0V75_S1_SRAM (0.75V) from SPMU BUCK 5 [page 108]; PP0V75_S1_VDD_FIXED (0.75V) from SPMU LDO 12 [page 110]; PP0V5_S1_SOC (0.5V) from MPMU BUCK 2 [page 108] ... and 2 more
steps.if_missing: SoC retention failure. Check specific rail for short, then check SoC U6000 BGA.

steps.action: Check AWAKE (DVFS) rails: Measure SoC dynamic voltage rails. These only appear when PMU_ACTIVE_READY is asserted.
steps.measure: PPVDD_MAME_PCPU (Variable (~0.7-1.1V)) from MPMU BUCK 0 [page 108]; PPVDD_MAME_ECPU (Variable (~0.5-0.9V)) from MPMU BUCK 11 [page 108]; PPVDD_MAME_GPU (Variable (~0.5-1.0V)) from MPMU BUCK 1 [page 108] ... and 3 more
steps.if_missing: none_present: PMU_ACTIVE_READY never asserted. Check PMU_RESET_L, SOC_FORCE_DFU signals (page 39-40).; some_present: Specific DVFS buck failure. Check output for short. May be SoC BGA issue.

steps.action: Check NAND/SSD power: If SoC appears to start but no boot: check NAND power rails.
steps.measure: PP1V25_MAME_NAND_VCC (1.25V) from SPMU BUCK 6 [page 108]; PP3V8_MAME_NAND_VDDL (3.8V) from SPMU BUCK 12 SW6/7 [page 109]; PP1V25_MAME_NAND_VCC2 (1.25V) from SPMU BUCK 13 SW5 [page 109]
steps.if_missing: NAND has no power. Check SPMU BUCK 6/12/13 outputs. If shorted, NAND IC may be dead.

steps.action: Check Display / DFR power: If board boots (current draw pattern suggests boot) but no display.
steps.measure: PPBUS_AONSW_LCD (12.6V) from Load switch from PPBUS_AON [page 79]; PPVOUT_LCDBKLT (Variable (backlight)) from LCD backlight boost [page 79]; PP5V_SM_LCD (5V) from From PP5V_S2 via display connector [page 78] ... and 2 more
steps.if_missing: no_backlight: Check PPBUS_AONSW_LCD load switch enable. Check backlight boost IC.; no_panel_power: Check display connector JP600 (page 78) for damage. Check PP5V_SM_LCD.; no_dfr: Check DFR_PWR_EN enable signal. Check UT700/UT710 for damage.

steps.action: Check USB-C / Thunderbolt: If board boots but USB-C ports not working.
steps.measure: PP5V_S2_UPC (5.23V) from UF800 [page 70]; PP1V2_USBC_VDDIO (1.2V) from UF900 [page 71]; PPVD055_USBC_CIO (0.855V) from UF939 [page 71]
steps.if_missing: ATC regulator failure. Check UF800/UF900/UF939 for damage.
